commands.author: KatTate

target.title: fix(acp): assign distinct session keys to Discord threads under the same parent channel
target.body:
## Summary

- **Root cause**: `matchDiscordAcpConversation` returned the parent channel ID as `conversationId` on a parent-match, and `resolveMaterializedConfiguredBinding` materialised using `resolved.match` (which carried that wrong ID). All threads under the same parent folded into one ACP session.
- **Fix**: parent-match now returns `{ matchPriority: 1 }` only; the registry materialises from the live `conversation` ref (threadId + parentConversationId) instead of the match object.
- **Type debt (Finding 3)**: narrowed `ChannelConfiguredBindingMatch` from `ChannelConfiguredBindingConversationRef & { matchPriority?: number }` to `{ matchPriority?: number }` — only `matchPriority` is consumed downstream; the identity fields were dead weight that obscured the original bug. Updated all 7 `matchInboundConversation` implementations and test mocks.
- **Known limitation (Finding 2)**: `configured-binding-session-lookup` uses `rule.target` (compile-time parent channel ID) for reverse lookup, so per-thread session keys can never be matched. Documented with a comment and a test that asserts `null`.
- **Pre-existing lint/type fixes** surfaced by staging the touched files (not introduced by this PR): `no-redundant-type-constituents` in `discord/channel.ts` and `feishu/channel.ts`; `TS2493` tuple-length mismatch in `msteams/attachments.test.ts`.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related: #50955 (complementary fix — expects `conversationId = threadId` which this PR ensures)
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause

- Root cause: `matchDiscordAcpConversation` (discord/channel.ts) returned `{ conversationId: parentConversationId, matchPriority: 1 }` on parent match. `resolveMaterializedConfiguredBinding` (configured-binding-registry.ts) then called `materializeConfiguredBindingRecord({ conversation: resolved.match })` which used that wrong `conversationId` for session key hashing. All threads under the same parent produced the identical session key.
- Missing detection: no test exercised two threads under the same parent and compared their session keys.
- Contributing context: `ChannelConfiguredBindingMatch` extended `ChannelConfiguredBindingConversationRef`, making the identity fields look meaningful in a match context when they were only used for priority selection.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: unit tests
- Target: `src/channels/plugins/acp-bindings.test.ts`
- New test: `"assigns distinct session keys to different threads under the same parent channel"` — creates two binding resolutions for thread IDs `1111111111111111111` and `2222222222222222222` under the same parent `1479098716916023408`, asserts `thread1.targetSessionKey !== thread2.targetSessionKey`.
- Also: `"returns null for per-thread session keys via reverse lookup"` — documents the known limitation that reverse lookup can't reconstruct thread-derived session keys.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Each Discord thread now gets its own independent ACP session rather than sharing the session of the parent channel.

## Diagram

N/A.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22 / pnpm
- Integration/channel: Discord / ACP

### Steps

1. Configure an ACP binding pointing at a Discord parent channel.
2. Send messages from two different threads under that parent.
3. Observe that each thread now routes to its own ACP session.

### Expected

Each thread has a distinct `targetSessionKey`.

### Actual (before fix)

All threads under the same parent shared the parent's session key.

## Evidence

- [x] Unit test passes: 8/8 in `acp-bindings.test.ts` including the new regression test
- [x] 928 discord extension tests pass
- [x] tsgo clean (only pre-existing `msteams` error fixed as part of this PR)

## Human Verification

- Verified: `pnpm vitest run src/channels/plugins/acp-bindings.test.ts` (8/8), `pnpm vitest run extensions/discord/src/` (928/928), `pnpm tsgo` clean.
- Edge cases checked: wildcard account binding, exact account binding, registry version invalidation.
- Not verified: live Discord end-to-end (requires deployed environment).

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes — sessions that previously shared a key will now split. Existing sessions under the parent channel ID continue to work; thread sessions start fresh.
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: existing threads that were silently sharing the parent session will now get a new session on next message. Any in-progress ACP state in the shared session is not migrated to per-thread keys.
  - Mitigation: this is the correct behaviour — threads should never have shared state with other threads or the parent channel.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
